The attack on Nakatomi Plaza

TW: Non-Con and Dead Dove, Death

Die Hard is a Christmas movie...

On Christmas Eve 1988, the sprawling Nakatomi Plaza towered over Los Angeles, its reflective glass gleaming like polished steel beneath the moonlight. Inside, a lavish holiday party pulsed with laughter and clinking glasses, the festive atmosphere a stark contrast to the bitter cold outside. Yet, lurking in the shadows of this modern citadel was Hans Gruber, a man as meticulously calculated as the finest Swiss watch, his mind sharp and his intentions lethal.

Gruber stood near the towering Christmas tree in the lobby, surveying the gathered executives and their families. He blended into the festive ambiance with his tailored suit and unassuming demeanor, but beneath the surface, his heart raced with anticipation. The night was not about merriment but meticulously planned chaos. With a team of highly trained mercenaries ready to execute his orders, this night was destined to become legendary.

As the clock ticked towards midnight, Hans exchanged a brief glance with Karl, one of his most trusted accomplices. Their eyes spoke volumesтАФtonight would end with the world watching. Gruber signaled to his men, and they slipped silently into position.

тАЬNow,тАЭ he murmured, voice smooth like velvet. His German accent, deliberate and crisp, commanded attention. He stepped forward, a gun concealed beneath his coat, ready to seize control.

The atmosphere shifted. Screams pierced the air as his men stormed the lobby, weapons drawn. Glass shattered, and panic erupted among the guests. тАЬLadies and gentlemen,тАЭ Hans called out, his voice booming, silencing the chaos with an icy calm, тАЬIтАЩm afraid your party has been abruptly interrupted. Please remain where you are.тАЭ

The executives froze, their faces a mixture of fear and disbelief. Hans reveled in the power he wielded, each breath savoring the moment. He was not merely robbing them; he was elevating this night to a grand stage, a performance of audacity and strategy.
